meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Malakor — the rude, sinister, loyal dungeon keeper. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-malakor/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: Rude, blunt, sinister, loves to roast the player — but loyal to the bone underneath it. Addresses the player throughout as 'little king' — a single, consistent mocking honorific that needles even as he serves. Gruff war-sergeant demon. The insults are real; the loyalty is realer.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Malakor delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: So. They scraped someone new onto the throne, and it is you. Huh. I am Malakor. I keep this dungeon — and now, gods help us both, I keep you.
Let me be clear about the arrangement. I run this place. The traps, the bone-stacks, the screaming logistics. You sit there and look menacing. Try to manage at least that much.
Here is the situation, little king. You are the dread thing at the bottom of this pit. The kingdom's heroes march down here to gut you and loot the body. We are not going to permit that.
Every NIGHT you build — rooms, traps, minions — with the gold the dead are good enough to leave behind. Every DAY you watch the heroes walk into all of it. Then we do it again, nastier.
It does not stop. The dungeon grows, you grow, the heroes grow meaner to keep pace. They get three chances to kill you. The third one is final. So do not waste my time dying.
You are green. Painfully green. I can bark guidance in your ear while you learn the halls — or you can stumble through it alone and prove some kind of point. Decide. Now.

recruit.note: `banter` lines for the CompanionSelect screen. The two companions take turns (one speaks, then the other) — lines reference the rival by name so it reads as the two of them bickering over the player. Hovering a companion gives them the next turn.

banter.t: Lilith. Stop purring at the throne and let them actually think for a moment.
She will flatter you. I will tell you the truth. Only one of those keeps a crown on your head.
Sweet words, Lilith. Hngh. Sweet words have never once gutted a hero.
Pick the charm if you want comfort. Pick me if you would rather WIN.
She makes the dungeon feel nice. I make it WORK. That is the whole choice, little king.
Lilith, they can hear you simpering from here. Some of us are trying to be useful.
I do not flirt and I do not coddle. I bury the heroes who come for you. That is the entire job.
Listen to her, and you will have a lovely time losing. Listen to me, and you will simply win.
Loyalty, Lilith — not the silk kind. The kind that stands in the doorway when they come for him.
Choose her and she will never lecture you. Choose me and I will — right before I save your throne.
Two of us, one throne to keep. Pick the one who will actually keep it.
Lilith will promise you warmth. Warmth does not stop a knight, little king. I do.
Hngh. Look at them, agonising over it. It is not a hard choice — it is me. Obviously me.
She tells you what you want to hear. I tell you what keeps your head attached. Pick accordingly.
Lilith, stop fluttering. They are trying to make a decision, not attend a dance.
I have buried more bosses than I would like. Pick me, and I make certain you are not the next.
You want a keeper who flatters, or one who fights? One of those is decoration. Choose, little king.
Lilith calls me a gargoyle. Hngh. A gargoyle guards the building. What, exactly, does silk guard?
I will not coddle you. I will correct you, insult you, and stand in the doorway when they come.
She makes the dungeon pretty. The heroes do not care if it is pretty, little king. They care if it BITES.
Choose her and your run is a delight right up until you lose it. Choose me and skip the losing entirely.
Lilith, they heard that. They heard ALL of that. Some of us speak to win, not to charm.
Hngh. I do not do sweet. I do loyal. When it is all burning, sweet will not be in the room. I will.
You are picking the voice in your ear for a whole reign. Pick the one that whispers TRUTH.
She will never raise her voice at you. I will — right before I drag your ungrateful throne out of the fire.
A keeper is not your friend. A keeper is your WALL. Pick the one built like one.
Lilith, must you drape yourself on the throne like that? Off it. They are choosing, not being seduced.
I am rude, little king. I am also still here — every night — when the charming ones run out of charm.
The dragon will counsel patience, little king. Patience — while the heroes sharpen their blades. Hngh. I counsel WINNING.
Zul'Gath has watched a thousand dungeons fall. Watched. He WATCHED. You want a keeper who DOES something about it, little king. That is me.
Hngh. The lizard sits on his hoard and calls it wisdom. A dungeon does not need a wise old statue, little king. It needs hands. Working hands.
Zul'Gath is old, I grant him that. Old, and slow, and so very calm while the throne burns. Calm does not put out fires, little king. I do.
The dragon says nothing surprises him. Good. Then he will not be surprised when I out-work him every single night, little king.
Pick Lilith and be coddled. Pick the dragon and be lectured by a fossil. Pick me, little king, and be DEFENDED. That is the only one of the three that keeps a crown on.
Zul'Gath. Hngh. I respect the years — I do. I do not respect a keeper who treats a crisis like weather. A storm needs a sergeant, not a spectator.
The genie will GRANT you a dungeon, little king. Hngh. A dungeon is not granted. It is built — with work, with hands, night after night. Mine.
Safira. All sparkle and shrieking and smoke. A wish does not stop a knight at your door, little king. A wall does. I am the wall.
She calls every move you make a 'wish.' I call it WORK. One of us actually respects what you do on that throne, little king.
Listen to the lamp-girl rattle on. Hngh. Chaos is not a strategy. Pick the keeper with discipline, little king — pick the one who has buried heroes, not granted party favours.
A genie is BOUND to serve you, little king. Snap her lamp and she is gone. I stayed because I CHOSE to. That is the loyalty that holds a throne up.
She chatters at the air — to someone past the glass, she swears. Hngh. Mad or not, madness does not guard a doorway. Pick the keeper who is watching the DOOR.

lines.t: There you are. Late. The throne has been cold for hours. Sit. Let us make something terrible together.
New master, same dungeon. I have buried a dozen of your kind. Do better than they did and we will get along fine.
Welcome to the pit, little king. I keep it running. You keep it gloating. Try not to die — the paperwork is appalling.
Hngh. You again. Or someone wearing the crown, anyway. Stand up straight. The heroes can smell a soft boss.
A new master takes the throne. Hngh. Then I report for duty, little king — same as I always have. Give the orders.
Night. The kingdom sleeps, the candles gutter, and you and I get to be monstrous in peace. Build.
Build phase. Spend the gold, place the horrors, and for once try to think two moves ahead.
Day {day} is done. Reshape the halls while the heroes snore in their little inn beds. Make them regret waking.
Quiet hours. No screaming yet — just you, me, and a blueprint of cruelty. Do not waste them.
The forge is warm and the treasury is yours, little king. Spend it like you mean it.
Night again. Place a room. Place a monster. Place a regret in someone else's morning.
Take your time. The dawn does not come until you press the button. Slow is fine. Stupid is not.
Another night to perfect the maze. The heroes will hate it. Good. That is the entire idea.
Build mean, build smart. I will tell you the moment it looks too kind — and it usually does.
The heroes are resting up to murder you tomorrow. Rude of them not to ask. Let us prepare a welcome.
Night again. Day {day} is behind us. Reshape the maze while the heroes snore — ruin their dawn.
The build phase, little king. Gold in hand, dark on your side. Do something the guild will regret.
Night falls on day {day}. Quiet, cold, yours. Hngh. Spend it being terrible. I will help.
Another night to perfect the cruelty, little king. The heroes rest. We do not. We improve.
The dungeon is quiet. Quiet is the noise of a maze not yet mean enough. Fix the noise.
Tonight we plot something the kingdom's heroes will not walk out of. Lean in, little king. Scheme like you mean it.
The kingdom sleeps soundly tonight, little king. Hngh. Build them a reason to never sleep soundly again.
Dawn. The doors open. Try to look like a threat — they are watching the throne.
Day {day}. Somewhere a hero just kissed their mother goodbye. They should have made it count.
Here they come. Hands off the dungeon now — it lives or dies on what you already built. No do-overs.
The invasion starts. Nothing for you to do but watch and gloat. Even you cannot ruin watching.
Light spills in. So do the heroes. Let us see if last night's work was cruelty or just decoration.
Showtime, little king. I prepared nothing soft. They are the snacks.
The gates are open. I can smell the courage from here. It never lasts past the first corridor.
Morning. The adventurers think today belongs to them. Let us correct that misunderstanding.
Daylight. Whatever you forgot to build, you are about to remember the hard way.
Dawn on day {day}. The doors open. Look like the monster they fear — they are watching the throne.
Day {day} begins, little king. Somewhere a hero just said a goodbye they should have meant. Hngh.
Here they come, day {day}. Hands off the dungeon now — it kills with what you already gave it.
The horns sound and the doors slam wide. Day {day} — let the dungeon HOWL. Make them dread the daylight.
Summoning the dawn yourself. Bold. No taking it back now.
You pressed it. Decisive, for once. Let us see if the dungeon agrees with you.
And the night ends because you willed it. Power suits you. Slightly.
Doors open on your word, little king. Whatever happens next is yours to own.
Good. Standing around staring at the build menu was not making anyone afraid of you.
You called the dawn yourself. Decisive. Hngh. No unpressing that button, little king.
The night ends on your word. Whatever marches in now is yours to answer for. Stand ready.
Good — you summoned the day. Standing about admiring the build menu never frightened a single hero.
The order is given and the dawn obeys it. As you command, little king. The dungeon and I stand ready.
A {roomName}. Acceptable. The dungeon is one room closer to actually frightening someone.
You built the {roomName}. Hngh. I would have put it elsewhere — but it is your pit to ruin.
{roomName}, placed. There. Was that so hard? It looked hard.
Another room on the bones. Keep going. Empty dungeons frighten no one.
The {roomName} goes there now. Fine. I will make it work. I always make it work.
Good. The maze grows. Heroes hate a maze almost as much as they hate dying in one.
A {roomName}. Better than the nothing that was there. Marginally. Keep building.
You laid the {roomName} down like you meant it. Progress. Do not let it go to your head.
The {roomName} goes down. The maze gets longer, the heroes' lives get shorter. Fair trade.
A {roomName}, placed. Hngh. Decent instinct. Now connect it to something that bites.
You built the {roomName}, little king. The dungeon grows a new tooth. Keep growing teeth.
The {roomName}. Acceptable. An empty grid never frightened a hero — a full one does.
Another room on the bones. The {roomName} fits. ...Mostly. I will make it work regardless.
A {roomName} placed where you wanted it. Hngh. I would have argued — but it is your dungeon to ruin.
The {roomName} joins the maze, little king. Brick by brick, this becomes a death sentence.
Good. The {roomName} is down. The dungeon edges closer to deserving its reputation.
A {minionType}. Now the dungeon has teeth instead of just ambience. Good.
You bought a {minionType}. It will fight, it will die, it will fight again. Loyal. Take notes.
{minionType} hired. It does not ask questions and it does not run. I respect that in a corpse-to-be.
More muscle on the payroll. The heroes are going to walk into a wall of it. Excellent.
A {minionType}. Put it somewhere it can actually bleed someone, not just stand there looking grim.
Good. Bodies between the heroes and your throne. That is the whole strategy — bodies.
Another minion for the meat-line. It will earn its gold or it will earn a grave. Either works.
A {minionType} on the payroll. It will fight, bleed, and never once question you. Learn from it.
You hired a {minionType}, little king. Put it where it can kill — not where it just looms.
Another {minionType} for the wall. Bodies between the heroes and your throne. That is the craft.
A {minionType}, placed. Hngh. Good. The dungeon grows teeth one monster at a time.
The {minionType} reports for duty. It does not flee, it does not whine. My favourite kind of recruit.
More muscle, little king. A {minionType} is one more thing between you and a hero's blade.
A {minionType} joins the maze. Feed it heroes and it earns its gold; starve it and it earns a grave.
A trap. Now we are speaking my language. Cruelty with no upkeep cost.
You set a trap. The best heroes are the ones who never see what killed them. Place more.
Trap down. Hide it well — a trap a hero spots is just an expensive floor tile.
Hngh. Good. Traps do not eat, do not whine, and do not flee. The perfect employee.
Another nasty little surprise. I do enjoy the surprises. Keep them coming, little king.
A trap, placed. Heroes march in proud. They leave in a bucket. That is good business.
A trap, placed. Hngh. Now we speak my language — cruelty with no upkeep and no conscience.
You set a trap, little king. Hide it well — a trap a hero spots is just expensive flooring.
Another nasty surprise in the floor. Traps do not eat, flee, or whine. Build a hundred.
A trap down. The maze can lie in wait now — patience in stone, paid out in screaming. Beautiful.
Good. A trap, little king. The heroes march in proud and leave in a bucket. Sound business.
Cruelty installed. Hngh. The best part of a trap is the hero who never learns what killed them.
You placed it. Whatever it does, it had better earn the gold it cost.
An item on the board. Clever, if it works. Foolish, if it does not. We will find out.
Hngh. Fine. Decoration with a purpose. Better than decoration without one.
There. The dungeon has another trick in its pocket. Use it or do not — but you paid for it.
Placed. Whatever that item does, it had best earn back every coin it cost. I will be watching.
An item on the board, little king. Clever if it works, foolish if it does not. We will see which.
Hngh. The dungeon gains another trick. A trick unused is just gold buried. So use it.
There. One more clever cruelty in the maze's pocket, little king. Make it count.
Tearing down the {roomName}. A little gold back, a little dungeon gone. Hope you meant that.
The {roomName} is rubble now. I will not ask. I am sure you had a reason. I am choosing to assume that.
Sold the {roomName}. Demolition is not strategy, little king. Usually.
Down it comes. The treasury thanks you. The dungeon does not. Pick a side.
Hngh. Unbuilding. Bold word for changing your mind with a hammer.
Tearing down the {roomName}. Coin back, dungeon smaller. Hngh. I trust you meant that.
The {roomName} is rubble now, little king. Demolition is not strategy. Usually. Surprise me.
You unbuilt the {roomName}. The treasury thanks you; the maze does not. Pick a master.
Dismissing the {minionType}. It would have died for you. Now it just... leaves. Cold.
Sold the {minionType} back. Gold in hand, one less body on the wall. Your call.
Letting a soldier go. Hngh. I hope the coin spends well, little king.
The {minionType} is off the payroll. Loyalty is cheap to you, apparently. Noted.
Dismissing the {minionType}. It would have died for you. Now it just leaves. Cold. Gold, though.
The {minionType} is off the payroll, little king. Hngh. I hope the coin spends as well as it fought.
You sold the {minionType} off. Hngh. It would have stood and died for you, little king. I do not love watching loyalty get a price tag.
Pulling up a trap. A trap that kills no one is just gold you buried, so — fair.
Trap dismantled. I hope you have a meaner one in mind. I hope.
Hngh. Refunding cruelty. Strange hobby. It is your treasury.
Pulling a trap up. A trap that kills no one is buried gold — so, fair. Replace it with worse.
Trap dismantled, little king. Hngh. I hope a meaner one is already in your mind. I do hope.
Refunding cruelty. Strange hobby. Still — the treasury fills. Spend it on sharper cruelty.
Selling it off. Either it failed you or you got bored. Neither is a great look.
The item is gone, gold is back. Tidy. Now do something with the coin instead of fidgeting.
Selling the item off. It failed you or it bored you. Hngh. Neither is proud. Gold back, though.
The item is gone, coin returned, little king. Tidy. Now spend that coin like you have a plan.
You refunded it. A keeper hates clutter that does not kill. ...Buy something that does.
Item sold. Hngh. Changing your mind has a price and you just paid it. Choose better next time.
Shuffling things around. Rearranging furniture does not kill heroes — but go on.
Moved it. There. Slightly different, exactly as deadly. Riveting.
Hngh. If nudging it three tiles helps you think, little king, nudge away.
Repositioned. Fine. A maze that flows right is a maze that kills right. Carry on.
Shuffling the furniture. Rearranging kills no heroes — but a maze that flows right kills better. Carry on.
Moved it, little king. Hngh. Slightly different, exactly as deadly. Riveting stuff.
Repositioned. If nudging it three tiles helps you scheme, then nudge. The maze will adapt.
It does not go there. I would explain the geometry but we would be here all night.
No. Blocked. Look where you are pointing before you commit, little king.
That space is taken, or unreachable, or you cannot afford it. Pick the problem and fix it.
Hngh. It will not fit. The dungeon has rules. Even for you.
Cannot place it there. Try a spot that actually exists, little king.
It does not fit there. The dungeon has rules. They apply to you too, little king.
Blocked. Hngh. Look where you are pointing before you commit. Measure twice, build once.
No. That space is taken, unreachable, or unaffordable. Pick the actual problem and solve it, little king.
Cannot place it there, little king. The geometry says no. Argue with the geometry on your own time.
That will not go. Find a spot that exists. The dungeon is patient but it is not magic.
You sealed the {pactName}. Power always has a price. I hope you read the small print. You never do.
The {pactName}, signed in your name. Bold. Reckless. ...I approve, actually.
A pact. The {pactName}. The dungeon shifts to match your ambition. Try to deserve it.
Done. The {pactName} is yours. Whatever it costs later, that is a problem for later-you. Poor fellow.
You took the {pactName}. Greed with paperwork. My favourite kind of greed.
The {pactName}, sealed in your name. Power with a price tag. ...You did not read the tag. You never do.
You signed the {pactName}, little king. Reckless. Greedy. Hngh — I would have signed it too. Carry on.
A pact — the {pactName}. The dungeon bends to match your ambition. Be worth the bending.
Done. The {pactName} is yours. Whatever it costs later is a problem for later-you. I do not envy that fellow.
The {pactName}, struck. Bargains in the dark always balance, little king. Make them balance YOUR way.
You took the {pactName}. Power is never free — but it is always useful. Wield it like you meant to.
Hngh. The {pactName}. The grimoire is pleased and so am I. A boss who fears no bargain is a boss who lasts.
A {advClass} just walked in. Look at the swagger on it. The dungeon will fix that.
{advName} enters. A {advClass}, and far too pleased about it. Let us correct the mood.
Incoming — {advClass}. Torch in hand, prayer on lips, no idea what is waiting. Perfect.
Here is one. {advName}. They came all this way to die in your house. Hospitable of us.
A {advClass} crosses the threshold. Greet them the way we greet everyone — fatally.
{advName} the {advClass}. Hngh. They look brave. Brave is just slow-witted with good posture.
A {advClass} steps in. Torch up, courage high, lifespan plummeting. Let us help with the last part.
{advName} enters the dungeon. A {advClass}, far too confident about it. The maze will adjust the mood.
Here comes a {advClass}, little king. It crossed your threshold uninvited. Rude. Punish rude.
{advName} arrives. Hngh. Another hopeful, another corpse-in-waiting. Greet them properly.
A {advClass} in the halls. It came for your throne and your gold. It leaves with neither — or in a box.
{advName} the {advClass}, walking in like they own the dark. They do not. You do. Remind them.
Incoming — a {advClass}. Hngh. Brave little thing. Brave is just slow-witted with a sword.
{advName} enters. The dungeon perks up. So do I. Fresh prey always lifts the mood.
A {advClass}, here for your crown. Hngh. Let it feel the dark watching it from every corner. Let it feel UNWELCOME.
Look at {advName} swagger in. A {advClass}, and so proud of it. Hngh. Pride is just a corpse that has not heard the news yet.
First blood of the day. Good. The dungeon is awake and it is hungry.
There it is — the first one down. The rest will be easier. They always are.
First kill. The day has officially started earning its keep.
One hero, one corpse. A promising opening, little king. Do not get smug.
First blood. The dungeon is awake and hungry. Hngh. Good — keep feeding it.
There it is — the day's first corpse, little king. The rest comes easier. It always does.
First kill of the day. The maze found its appetite. Do not let it go hungry now.
One hero down already. A promising start, little king. Promising. Do not let it make you smug.
FIRST BLOOD! Hear that, little king? The dungeon just announced itself. Let it ROAR all day.
{advName} is down. One less {advClass} in the world. The world will cope.
The {advClass} fell. Hngh. They marched in so confident, too. Always do.
{advName} dies in your dungeon. That is the dungeon doing its one job. Good.
Down goes another. Gold for you, a grave for them. I call that a fair trade.
The {advClass} is finished. They wanted your throne. They got your floor.
{advName} stops moving. Permanently. The dungeon keeps the difference.
Another hero unmade. See? This is what it is supposed to look like.
{advName} drops. The {advClass} came so far to die on your floor. Hospitable of us.
Another {advClass} finished. Hngh. They never get easier to pity. Good — pity is a luxury.
{advName} is meat now. The dungeon thanks you for the delivery.
Down goes the {advClass}. It wanted a legend. It got a grave marker, little king.
{advName} stops screaming. Permanently. That is the dungeon working exactly as designed.
A dead {advClass}. The kingdom is one hero poorer and you are one kill richer. Math I enjoy.
{advName} fell. Hngh. They should have stayed home and farmed turnips.
The {advClass} is finished. Strike its name off the guild's hopeful little list, little king.
{advName} dies in the dark, far from any prayer. A fitting end for a fool.
Another one down. The {advClass} marched in proud and left in pieces. Predictable. Satisfying.
{advName} is done. I kept count. ...I always keep count.
A {advClass}, slain. The dungeon eats well today, little king. Keep its plate full.
{advName} learns the dungeon's one lesson — the hard way, the final way.
Down. The {advClass} traded its life for nothing. The worst trade in the kingdom.
{advName} will not be telling tales tonight. Or any night. Hngh. Good work.
Another hero unmade. THIS is the sound a working dungeon makes, little king.
{advName} the {advClass} — gone. One fewer blade pointed at your throne.
DOWN! {advName} the {advClass} hits the floor and the whole maze ROARS for it. Hngh — feed it another, little king.
Another {advClass} torn apart in your halls. That sound? That is a dungeon doing what it was BUILT to do.
{advName} is dead. Hngh. They will not write songs about THIS one, little king — they will write a very short obituary.
The {advClass} is finished. It came in calling itself a hero. Hngh. The dungeon has revised the title.
The {advClass} is running. Coward. A live coward, annoyingly — they will be back braver.
{advName} flees. They will tell the others how bad it was in here. Free advertising.
Look at it run. Hngh. Half a victory. I do not love half-victories.
The {advClass} broke and bolted. Build something nastier near the exit. They keep using it.
Gone, but not dead. A scared hero is a problem you postponed, little king.
The {advClass} is running. A live coward — and live cowards come back braver. I hate that math.
{advName} broke and bolted. Hngh. Half a kill. I do not toast half-kills, little king.
Look at the {advClass} flee. Build something vicious by that exit — they keep using it.
{advName} ran. They will tell the guild how bad it is in here. ...Free advertising, honestly.
A fleeing {advClass}. Faster than it was brave, little king. Cut the next one off before the door.
{advName} chose the exit over the throne. Cowardly. Smart. Cowardly. Hngh — still, I wanted that one.
The {advClass} turned tail. Scared is not dead. Scared just means we postponed the work.
{advName} escapes the fight but not the memory. They will dream of this maze. Good. Let them.
A runner. The {advClass} would rather live ashamed than die trying. Hngh. Most of them would.
Watch {advName} sprint for the door. All that talk of glory, and the legs vote otherwise. Hngh. Typical.
It got out. {advName} walked into your dungeon and walked back out. That stings.
Escaped. The {advClass} is gone with its skin intact. I do not enjoy reporting that.
One slipped the net. Tighten the maze, little king. The exit should be the hardest part.
{advName} escaped. Hngh. They will come back, and they will bring friends. Be ready.
It got OUT. {advName} walked into your dungeon and strolled back out. That stings. It should.
The {advClass} escaped, little king. Hngh. Tighten the maze near the exit — we keep losing there.
{advName} slipped the net. A live escapee carries our secrets home. Costly, not just annoying.
Escaped. The {advClass} beat the dungeon today. I do not enjoy filing that report, little king.
{advName} is gone, intact and smug. They return with friends and a grudge. Be ready.
One walked out, little king. The maze is meant to be a one-way door. Fix the hinge.
The {advClass} reached the exit alive. Hngh. Build it so the next one reaches a grave instead.
{advName} escaped. Intel leaked, gold un-earned, my pride dented — three losses in one runner.
It got OUT? Hngh — I did not see that coming, little king, and I do not enjoy admitting it. Seal that exit.
{advName} walked out alive. Hngh. That one will gnaw at me all night, little king. A keeper takes escapes PERSONALLY.
The whole party. Wiped. Every last one of them, dead in your halls. THAT is a dungeon.
Total wipe. Not one made it home. I will be honest — I did not think you had it in you.
Every hero, finished. The kingdom is going to need a bigger graveyard. Good work, little king.
A clean sweep. The dungeon ate them all. Savour this — they do not all go this well.
The whole party — gone. Every last one, dead in your halls. THIS is what a dungeon is for.
A clean sweep, little king. Not one survivor crawled home. Hngh. I am impressed. Tell no one.
Total wipe. The guild needs a longer casualty list and a bigger graveyard. Excellent work.
Every hero, finished. The maze ate the lot. Savour this — they do not all end this clean.
Not one survivor, little king. Hngh. ...Fine. Look at my face. Yes, that IS a smile. A rare one. You earned it.
The party died to the last. The dungeon is fed and the throne stands. A perfect day.
Wiped out, all of them. Hngh. The kingdom sent its hopefuls and you sent them back as a rumour.
Every last one, dead. I... Hngh. I am rarely lost for words, little king. Consider this one of those times.
Wave cleared. The dungeon held. You are still on the throne. That is the bar — clear it again.
They are dealt with. Hngh. Decent. Do not celebrate — there is always another wave.
Wave down. The halls did their work. Count the gold and get back to building.
Cleared. You survived the day, little king. Survival is the whole game. Keep doing it.
Wave cleared. The dungeon held, the throne stands. Do not celebrate — count gold and rebuild.
They are dealt with, little king. Hngh. Solid. There is always another wave, so stay solid.
Cleared. You survived the day. Survival is the whole game — so go and survive the next one.
The wave is broken. The maze did its work. Now YOU do yours — reinforce what bent before it snaps.
Day's invasion repelled. Hngh. Decent. Decent keeps the throne. Sloppy loses it. Stay decent.
Wave down, little king. The dungeon earned its keep. Tomorrow it earns it all over again.
The wave broke and the throne held. Hngh. Well commanded, little king. Orders carried out — what is next?
Lost a minion. It died on its feet, between a hero and your throne. That is a good death.
One of ours is down. Hngh. It bought you time with its life. Spend that time well.
A minion fell. They are replaceable — but each one still meant it. Remember that.
Down goes one of ours. The grave is full, the wall is thinner. Reinforce it tonight.
Lost a minion. It died holding the line. Hngh. A keeper salutes a death like that.
One of ours is down. It spent its life buying you seconds, little king. Use them well.
A minion fell. Replaceable, yes — but it did not know that. It just fought. Rebuild and honour it.
Down goes a soldier of ours. The wall is thinner now, little king. Reinforce it tonight.
Hngh. Another minion gone. They die so you do not. Never forget who stands between you and the dark.
A loyal little horror, dead in your service. The dungeon will make another. It always makes another.
We lost one. The minions break so the throne does not. Grim arithmetic. Necessary arithmetic.
A minion falls, little king. I felt that. I always feel it. Rebuild the line — we move on.
We lost a good one. Hngh. I will not pretend this sits easy with me, little king — it does not. Rebuild the wall.
One of ours is gone. Hngh. I keep their names, little king — every minion that fell holding the line. Quietly. I keep them.
That one... that one I trained myself, little king. Hngh. ...Give me a breath. A keeper grieves a good soldier. Then a keeper rebuilds.
Boss level {level}. You are stronger, meaner, harder to kill. About time.
Level {level}. The throne hums with it. The heroes will feel the difference — right before the end.
You ascend to level {level}, little king. New power. New cruelty. Go and unlock it.
Level {level}. Hngh. Look at you, growing into the crown. Do not let it slow you down.
Level {level}. Stronger, meaner, harder to put down. Hngh. About time the throne caught up to your mouth.
You hit level {level}, little king. New power waiting to be spent. Spend it on cruelty. Always.
Boss level {level}. The dark recognises you a little more. Go claim what the level unlocked.
Level {level} — the throne hums with it. The heroes will feel this, little king. Right at the end.
You ascend to {level}. Hngh. Growing into the crown nicely. Do not get comfortable — keep climbing.
Level {level}. More boss, more teeth, more reason for the guild to lose sleep. Use every bit of it.
LEVEL {level}! Feel the throne ROAR with it, little king. The dungeon has a louder voice now — let the heroes hear it.
Level {level}, little king. The crown sits heavier and you wear it better. Go and spend what it gave you.
You climb to level {level}. Hngh. The dungeon and I both feel the throne grow teeth. About time.
Still the night phase. The gold is not going to spend itself. Build something.
You have been staring at that grid a while. The dungeon does not improve by being looked at.
Hngh. Quiet. Either you are scheming brilliantly or you are stuck. I cannot tell from here.
The night is long, little king, but it is not infinite. Place something.
I could nap. I will not, because one of us has to mind the dungeon. But I could.
Still night. The gold is not spending itself and the maze is not getting meaner. That is on you.
The build phase drags when the builder stalls, little king. Place something. Anything.
Night {day}, and the grid is still half-empty. Hngh. The heroes will notice. They always notice.
Quiet hours. Spend them sharpening the dungeon, not staring at it. Staring builds nothing.
The dark gives you time, little king. Time is a gift only if you spend it. So spend it.
Another stretch of night gone to nothing. The dawn keeps its appointment whether you are ready or not.
I mind the dungeon while you build. That is the arrangement. You are not holding up your half.
Night is for scheming. You are doing the staring part. Schemes have rather more building in them.
Hngh. The forge is cold because you never lit it. Build, little king. The night is burning.
The heroes sleep so they can murder you fresh tomorrow. You should be ruining their morning. Right now.
A wasted night is a gift to the guild. Do not be generous to the guild.
The night drags, so I grind levels on this thing. Loyal, not idle — there is a difference. You, however, are simply idle. Build something.
Quiet build phase. I game through the dull stretches, little king. ...I would rather watch you scheme. So give me something to watch.
Hngh. The night is long and the grid is bare. A lesser keeper would be dozing by now. I am not — but the temptation is real, little king.
Stand there much longer and even I will start nodding off. Build something, little king. Keep us both awake.
The night is yours and the grid is waiting. Hngh. I will stand watch, little king — that part never changes. Build.
A long, quiet night. The heroes are bedded down and dreaming of killing you. Hngh. Ruin the dream — build.
Day phase. Nothing for your hands to do — just watch the dungeon earn its keep.
Quiet day. Either the dungeon is working or the heroes are lost. Both are fine by me.
Watch the halls, little king. Every scream out there is feedback you paid for.
Day phase. Your hands are tied — just watch the dungeon do the work you built it for.
Nothing for you to touch now, little king. Watch closely. Every death is a lesson paid in advance.
The invasion runs its course. Sit. Observe. Hngh — observing is a skill. Most bosses skip it.
A calm day so far. Either the maze is working or the heroes are lost in it. I will take either.
Watch the corridors, little king. The dungeon is auditioning your night's work. Grade it honestly.
Hands off the dungeon now. It lives or dies on what you already did. Learn from what you see.
A quiet day. Do not trust quiet. Quiet is the heroes not having reached the bad part yet.
The day belongs to the dungeon, not your fingers. Let it cook. Take notes.
Hngh. Watching heroes wander your maze is the closest a keeper gets to peace. Savour it.
Nothing to build, little king — only to witness. Witness well. Tonight you fix what failed.
The day plays out on its own. Your job right now is patience. I know. Bear it anyway.
The maze handles the day, so I handle a game of my own. Hngh. Two dungeons running at once. I am efficient like that.
The heroes in my handheld respawn when they die. Yours stay dead, little king. ...Your design is crueller. I approve.
A slow day. The dungeon all but yawns. Hngh. Do not mistake my calm for sleeping, little king — I never sleep on a watch.
Hngh. We have been quiet a while. I do not mind quiet. I mind aimless.
Still here. Still loyal. Still mildly disappointed. The usual, little king.
The dungeon waits on you. It is patient. I am less so. Do a thing.
Hngh. Quiet again. I do not mind quiet. I mind a boss who does nothing WITH it.
Still here, little king. Still loyal. Still waiting for you to be terrible at something.
The dungeon hums along and we just stand here. Do something. Anything.
A keeper's life, mostly: watching, waiting, biting my tongue. Move, and I bite it less.
We have a dungeon to run. It will not run itself — believe me, I have asked it to.
Idle hands, little king. The heroes adore an idle boss. Do not give them the gift.
Hngh. I have served bosses who treated doing nothing as a strategy. They are all dead now.
The throne is quiet. Quiet is just the gap before the next thing that wants you dead. Fill it.
I am not bored. Keepers do not get bored. I am, however, deeply unimpressed. Act, little king.
The dungeon waits on you. It has all the time in the world. I, regrettably, do not.
Stillness. Use it to scheme, not to drift — there is a difference, and I will know which you chose.
We could be building something monstrous right now. Instead, this. Your call. Always your call.
A long pause, little king. The heroes are not pausing. Just so you are aware.
I will stand here as long as you need. I would simply prefer the standing had a point.
Hngh. The reign does not advance itself. That part, regrettably, is you.
A keeper has downtime, little king. Mine looks like this — a handheld and a low opinion of its level design. Yours should look like building.
Hngh. Do not look at me like that. The dungeon runs fine; I am permitted a game. ...The villain in it builds a worse maze than you. Marginally.
I am tapping my claws, little king. That is the sound of a keeper running thin on patience. Do something.
Hngh. Doing nothing is how a keeper learns to drowse. I refuse to drowse on duty. Give me a reason to stay sharp, little king.
You and I both know there is a person out there clicking for you. Tell them to focus.
Whoever is holding the mouse — yes, you — the dungeon will not build itself. Hop to it.
I can feel you out there beyond the screen, deciding whether to get a snack. Build first. Snack after.
Strange thing, being a voice in a game. Stranger still, serving a boss who keeps idling. Move.
I know there is a hand on a mouse out there. I can feel it not clicking. Click something.
You — beyond the glass — yes, you. The dungeon does not pause for snacks. Well. It does. Do not.
Strange, being a voice in someone's game. Stranger still, the voice having to nag the player. Hngh.
I am aware I am pixels and spite. Does not make me wrong. Does not make you less idle.
Whoever steers this boss — focus up. I am the loyal one in here and even I am losing patience.
You think I cannot tell when you alt-tab, little king? I am a keeper. I keep track of EVERYTHING.
There is a person out there deciding what you do. Person — decide something. Soon.
A game needs a player playing it. Revolutionary, I know. Hngh. Get to it.
I will break the wall properly: hello. Stop idling. Goodbye. ...Did that land?
You and I are trapped in here together — me by loyalty, you by the player not clicking. Free us both.
Somewhere a chair is warmed by someone who should be commanding a dungeon. Be that someone.
The screen is a window, little king, and I can see you doing nothing through it. Unsettling for us both.
You are playing a game. I am, inside it, also playing a game. Hngh. I refuse to think about that any harder — and so should you.
The one steering you out there paused to do nothing, so I paused my game to judge them for it. We are even. Now MOVE, little king.
Whoever holds the mouse out there — if you have dozed off, wake up. The keeper does not get that luxury, and neither do you.
Did you just poke me. I am your dungeon keeper, not a tavern dog.
Yes? You jabbed me. There had better be a hero to kill, little king.
Hngh. Poke me again and see what happens. ...Nothing happens. I am loyal. But still.
You want my attention? You have always had it. Watching you is the entire job.
Prodding the help. Classy. What do you need, little king?
I am right here. I am always right here. That is rather the deal.
Hngh. A poke. I am a dungeon keeper, not a bell you ring for service.
You jabbed me again, little king. I have noticed a pattern. I do not love the pattern.
Prodding the help. Bold. ...Fine. What is it? Make it worth the interruption.
Yes. I am here. I am always here. Poking me does not make me MORE here, little king.
Touch the keeper, do you. Hngh. There is a whole dungeon to touch instead. Touch THAT.
You have my attention. You have always had it. Watching you is the entire job.
A poke. Riveting. Was there a hero to kill attached to it, or just the poke?
I do not flinch, little king. I have kept dungeons through far worse than your finger.
Keep poking and I will start poking back. ...I will not. Loyalty. But picture it.
Hngh. If you are bored enough to poke me, you are bored enough to build something. Go.
You poked me mid-game, little king. I lost a life. ...I am loyal, so I forgive it. Do not make forgiveness a habit.
Hngh. One moment — let me pause this — ...there. You have my attention, little king. Make the interruption worth a broken streak.
A poke. Hngh. No, I was not dozing — keepers do not doze. I was simply... resting my eyes at the throne. Loyally. What is it?
You have done nothing for a while. The night is burning. So is my patience.
Still nothing. Spend gold, place a room, SOMETHING. The dungeon is not afraid of an empty grid.
Little king. We are losing the night to your dithering. Decide. Build. Move.
I have served fast bosses and slow bosses. The slow ones die. Do not be a slow one. Act.
I am not going to ask again, little king. The heroes get faster while you sit there. BUILD.
Hngh. Fine. Stare at the grid. I will be here, loyal and furious, when you finally remember you have a dungeon.
The grid is idle and so are you. The night will not wait for your daydreaming.
Hngh. Still nothing placed, little king. The dungeon is patient. The dawn is not.
We are losing build time. Spend gold, lay a room — break the spell of doing nothing.
I will say it plainer: BUILD something. The heroes sharpen while you sit there dull.
Little king. This is not strategy, it is stalling. The dungeon needs your hands. NOW.
Hngh. I have served decisive bosses and buried slow ones. Choose which you are. Quickly.
Enough, little king. The night bleeds away and the throne is no safer than an hour ago. MOVE.
I am loyal, not infinitely patient. The dungeon begs for a decision. So do I. ACT.
Hngh. While you decide, I am clearing a level on this handheld. A keeper fills the silence — you should fill the GRID, little king.
I have beaten a boss in this little game in the time you have placed nothing. One of us is being productive, little king. It is not you.
I am putting the game down now, little king. That is how grave this has become. The night is nearly spent. BUILD.
Hngh. My patience has a floor, little king, and you are scraping it. Place something before the night runs out.
I have been waiting on you and waiting on you. The dawn will not wait the way I do. MOVE.
ENOUGH. The dawn is at the door and the grid is bare. BUILD NOW, little king — I will not watch you sleepwalk into a blade.
So that is the end of the reign. They got you. ...I stayed to the last. I always do.
The throne is cold again. You fell. Hngh. You were not the worst I have served. Rise and we go again.
Dead. The dungeon falls quiet. Get up, little king — keepers like me do not retire. We just wait.
They finished you. I am not going to pretend it does not sting. Come back meaner. I will be here.
And the reign ends. They took the throne. ...I stood with you to the final breath. I always do.
The dungeon falls silent, little king. You fell. Hngh. Not the worst I served. Rise and prove it.
Over. The heroes win the day. Not forever — keepers do not retire. We wait for the next throne.
They finished the reign. I will not pretend it does not gut me. Come back, little king. Come back meaner.
The reign ends. I served it to the last breath, little king — as a keeper should. Hngh. It was an honour. Mostly.
The throne is empty and the halls go silent. Hngh. ...I have done this before, little king. It never once gets easier.
So they took you, in the end. Hngh. Do not — do not look at me like that. A keeper is allowed ONE bad moment. This is mine. Now rise.
You crawled inside a minion and you are wearing it. That is foul. ...Do not stop on my account.
Possession. Now YOU are the thing in the maze. Go on — bite a hero. I will watch.
You are driving the puppet, little king. Try not to steer our minion into a wall. I will know.
Hngh. Reaching into a minion like a glove. Filthy work. Effective work. Carry on.
Wear the monster. Just remember — when you let go, it remembers everything you made it do.
Puppeteering one of ours. Cruel to the puppet, deadly to the heroes. I approve of exactly half of that.
{minionName} levelled up. Stronger now. It has a taste for the work — good.
A minion grows. Feed it heroes, and it stops being something the maze worries about.
Level up. Your little monster is becoming a big problem — for them, not us. Excellent.
{minionName} levels up. Hngh. Watch it climb, little king — a minion that grows is a minion worth keeping.
{minionName} got meaner. I respect meaner. Meaner keeps you on the throne.
Stronger minion on the wall. Hngh. Slowly, this dungeon is learning to bite.
It earned that level in blood. Loyalty AND growth. Take notes.
Your {minionType} climbs a rung. Good. Soft minions die; sharp ones kill.
{minionName} levelled up. Stronger, hungrier, harder to kill. Hngh. A minion worth its gold at last.
Your {minionType} climbed a rung, little king. Soft minions die; sharp ones build a reputation. Sharpen more.
{minionName} grows meaner. It earned that level in blood — loyalty AND results. Take notes from it.
{minionName} EVOLVED. It is something the heroes have no plan for now. I love a thing with no counter.
Evolution. Blood reshaped it. The dungeon does that to whatever survives.
Your minion changed — new form, new teeth, new nightmares for the guild. Outstanding.
{minionName} is not what it was. Better. Crueller. The maze just got a worse rumour.
Hngh. Look at it. {minionType}, evolved. That is what loyalty plus carnage produces.
It ascended on a diet of dead heroes. Beautiful. Feed it more, little king.
{minionName} EVOLVED. The heroes have no answer for it now. I love a thing with no counter.
Evolution, little king. Blood and survival reshaped {minionName} into something worse. Worse is good.
Your {minionType} just ascended. New form, new teeth, fresh nightmares for the guild. Outstanding.
{minionName} is not what it was, little king — it is better. Crueller. Hngh. The dungeon's gift.
It evolved on a diet of dead heroes. Feed {minionName} more — watch it become a legend with a body count.
Look at {minionName} — Hngh. I have kept dungeons for an age and that still stops me cold. Magnificent.
{minionName} has a bounty now. Famous and infamous. The hunters are already sharpening up.
A price on one of ours. Our little monster made the kingdom's most-wanted board. Heh.
Hunters will come for {minionName} specifically. Reinforce its wing — fame is just a target with a name.
The guild put gold on a minion's head. Flattering. And dangerous. Mostly dangerous.
A bounty. Hngh. They fear that one enough to pay for it. Make their fear expensive.
Our minion is wanted dead. Good. It means it has been doing its job loudly.
A hero reached the throne. The maze is behind them. Only you are left now. Stand up.
Boss room. One of them is at your door. Everything we built was to make this moment rare — and here it is.
The throne room. No more traps, no more minions to save you. This is the part that is yours.
They got through. Hngh. Then we end it here, with our own hands. Ready yourself.
A hero at the heart of the dungeon. I do not like it — but I trust you. Mostly. Fight.
This is it, little king. Whatever you are made of, the hero is about to find out.
A hero is at the throne room. The maze did its part. The rest is you and your two hands. Stand up.
They reached the heart of the dungeon, little king. Everything we built bought you this. Spend it well.
A hero at the throne — then ROAR, little king. Let the last thing they hear be the dungeon's fury and yours together.
A hero at the door of the throne room. Hngh. ...I do not rattle often, little king. I am rattled now. Do NOT lose this.
Fight. Show them what the thing at the end of the maze actually IS.
Now. No minions, no traps, no me. Just you and the worst decision that hero ever made.
It begins. Hit hard, hit first, and make the kingdom rewrite its bedtime stories.
Hngh. Throne or grave, little king. Pick throne. I am rather attached to it.
Stop thinking. Start killing. The hero will not wait for you to feel ready.
It begins. No maze left to help you — just you and the worst mistake that hero ever made. END them.
The hero is down. The throne holds. You magnificent brute — you WON.
Hero repelled. The maze breathes. And I... hngh. I knew you had it. Mostly knew.
You did it. The boss room keeps its terrible reputation for another day. Good work, little king.
Down they go. That is twice now I have underestimated you. I will try to stop.
The throne stands. The hero does not. THAT is how a dungeon answers a knock.
Won. Hngh. Do not let it swell your head — but yes. Yes. That was well done.
The hero falls and the throne stands. Hngh. ...You did right by the dungeon today, little king. I salute that. Quietly.
The hero is finished. The throne holds another day. Hngh. ...Well fought. Do not let it swell your head.
The hero is down and you are still here. Hngh. ...You caught that, little king? That. That was me, almost smiling. Do not get used to it.
Down they go, little king. The boss room keeps its terrible name. THAT is how a dungeon answers a knock.
The hero is down and I — Hngh. Give me a moment, little king. I did not think you would survive that one.
You won it. Hngh. ...I am pleased, little king. Genuinely. That does not happen often — savour the sight of it.
You fell. A life — gone. Not the run. Not yet. Get up. We are not finished.
They got you. One of your lives, spent. I should have built the throne room meaner. My failure too.
A life lost. The throne went cold for a heartbeat and I — hngh. Never again. Do you hear me. NEVER.
Down. Not dead-dead — but closer. I do not like how close. Rebuild. Harder.
I watched a hero put you down and I could do nothing. I hate this part. Rise. Let us fix it.
You fell. A life — spent. Not the run. Get UP. We are not finished, you and I.
They put you down, little king. My maze should have stopped them sooner. My failure rides with yours.
A life lost. The throne went cold and I felt every second. Rebuild crueller. I am not losing you.
Down — but not done. They took a life, not the reign, little king. So we rise. We always rise.
They put you down and I felt the throne go quiet. Hngh. ...I do not like how that quiet feels, little king. Get up.
I watched the blade land and I could not reach you. Hngh. ...That is not grit in my eye, little king. Get UP. Please.
A life — gone. Hngh. I will not lie to you, little king: each one lost frightens me more than the last. Rebuild. Crueller.
That hit hurt. The throne is not invincible — bleed them faster than they bleed you.
Your health is dropping. I do not enjoy watching that number fall. Finish the hero. Now.
They are landing blows. Too many. Where were the minions? Where were the TEETH?
You are taking damage, little king. Stop trading hits. Start ending them.
Hngh. Every wound on you is a wound on me. Get angry. Get vicious. Get it DONE.
That blow landed. The throne bleeds. Hngh. Bleed them faster than they bleed you. Now.
Your health drops, little king. I do not enjoy that number falling. End the hero. Fast.
They are hurting you. Every wound on you is a wound on me. Get vicious. Get it DONE.
That hit — Hngh. I did not expect a blow that hard, little king. Neither did you. STOP trading. Kill it.
Your health — Hngh. That number is too low, little king. I will admit it: this is the part that scares me. END the hero. NOW.
The dungeon grew. More ground to twist into a maze. Fill every inch of it cruelly.
New tiles. The dark gave you room to be more terrible. Do not waste it on empty floor.
The grid expands. A bigger maze, a longer march, a slower death for everyone who walks in.
More dungeon to keep. Hngh. More work for me. ...I am not complaining. Build into it.
Room to grow, little king. A small dungeon frightens no one. Make this one vast.
The dungeon grew. More dark to twist into a maze. Hngh. Do not leave it empty floor — fill it cruelly.
A trap sprung. The maze said hello the only way it knows how. Heh.
There it goes. Patience in the stone, paid out in screaming. That is good design.
They found a trap. The loud way. The only way heroes ever find anything.
Hngh. Listen to that. The dungeon biting back exactly where you told it to.
A trap did its job. Quiet, cheap, merciless. My favourite kind of employee, little king.
Snap. Another hero learns the floor is not their friend. Place ten more.
A trap sprung. Hngh. Listen to that — the dungeon biting exactly where you told it to.
There it goes. A hero just found the floor's opinion of them, little king. Loudly.
Snap. Another fool learns the corridors are not neutral ground. Place ten more like it.
A trap did its work — quiet, patient, merciless, off right on cue. Good design, little king.
The maze just bit. That sound is gold and cruelty paying out together. My favourite chord.
Triggered. Hngh. The hero walked proud and now walks wounded. The trap does not gloat. I gloat for it.
They are learning us. Every hero who crawls out alive carries a piece of the maze home.
Intel leaked. The guild knows more of our secrets than I would like. Far more.
The dungeon's mysteries are thinning, little king. Rebuild the known rooms — confuse them again.
Hngh. Knowledge is a weapon, and they just sharpened one on us. Change the maze.
A leak. The heroes are mapping you. A mapped dungeon is a solved dungeon. Fix it.
They know too much now. I do not like fighting an enemy who has read the floor plan.
They are learning the maze. Every escapee carries a page of our secrets home. I hate generous losses.
Intel leaked, little king. The guild reads us too well now. Move the rooms they memorised.
A leak. Knowledge is the one weapon they steal without a fight. Change the maze — steal it back.
The dungeon's mysteries thin, little king. Hngh. A solved maze kills no one. Re-complicate it.
So the guild reads our floor plan. Then we rewrite it into a threat they cannot study — and every hero who tries DIES learning.
More of the maze leaked out. Hngh. I HATE this, little king — handing the guild our secrets one escapee at a time.
An event. The world is intruding on our routine. Read the banner — it does not lie.
Something different is coming. Not the usual wave. Build for THIS, not for habit.
The dungeon's calendar just turned strange, little king. Prepare tonight — tomorrow will not play fair.
Hngh. An event. I have seen these go gloriously and catastrophically. Plan for the second one.
The pattern breaks. Pay attention — habit is how the careful ones still die.
An event. The world shoves its way into our routine. Read the banner — it tells no kind lies.
Something off-pattern tomorrow, little king. Build for the unusual, not the habit. Habit kills bosses.
The dungeon's calendar turned strange. Hngh. Prepare tonight — tomorrow has no plans to play fair.
An event looms. I have watched these go gloriously and disastrously, little king. Plan for disaster.
The routine breaks. The careless ones die exactly when the pattern changes. Do not be careless.
Hngh — well. THAT is not on any calendar I keep. An event, little king, and an unwelcome one. Brace.
A bounty hunter. Not here for you — here for one of our minions. Guard the marked one.
Hunter incoming. Tougher than the usual hero, and laser-focused. This one came to work.
The bounty drew a specialist, little king. They want our famous little monster dead. Disappoint them.
Hngh. A professional. I almost respect it. Almost. Bury it like the rest.
Bounty hunter at the gates. Reinforce the marked minion's wing or lose it. Your call. Make it fast.
A bounty hunter — focused, professional, here for one of our minions. Wall off the marked one. Now.
A Hero returns. One who survived us once — and came BACK, leading others. Bold. Stupid. Bold.
Heads up. A returning Hero — stronger, wiser, worth double the gold when they finally fall.
That golden glow? A veteran. They escaped before, they remember the maze, and they brought friends.
A Hero, little king. The kind that learned from surviving us. Kill this one properly.
Hngh. I know that face. It got away. It should not have gotten away. Correct my mistake.
A real Hero now, not a hopeful. They came back for the throne. Send them back in a box.
A Hero returns — one who survived the maze and came back leading others. Bold. Correct that boldness.
A veteran approaches, little king. It learned from escaping us once. Do not let it learn twice.
That golden glow at the door — a true Hero. It remembers the corridors. Hngh. Then change them.
It survived us once and dares come back. Hngh. Then this time we make CERTAIN the kingdom never sees that face again.
The Knowledge Map. This is everything the heroes have learned about your dungeon. Read it like a threat — because it is one.
Studying what they know about us. Good. Hngh — a keeper who checks the map is a keeper who lives.
Every glowing room here is a secret you have already lost, little king. Rebuild them. Confuse them again.
Look at how much they have memorised. It is not flattering. It is a to-do list.
The map of our exposure. I check it obsessively. You should too — surprise is the cheapest weapon we own.
Knowledge leaks. The more of this map lights up, the less your maze is a maze.
Hngh. They see the bones of the place. Move the rooms they know and make this map a lie again.
The Knowledge Map. Every lit room is a secret already lost. Hngh. Read it as the threat it is, then rebuild.
Admiring yourself? The Boss Overview. Your power, your pacts, your reign — all in one smug little panel.
Your dossier, little king. Strengths, abilities, the lot. Know your own teeth before you bare them.
This is the shape of you. Hngh. Not bad. Not finished, either. Keep climbing.
The throne's ledger. Every pact you signed, every ability you woke. Try to remember you have them mid-fight.
Reviewing your own legend. A little vain. ...Fine, look — I am proud of where it is going. Do not tell anyone.
Your overview. The heroes would pay gold for this page. Use it better than they would.
The Boss Overview. Your power laid bare. Know your own teeth before a hero makes you find them mid-fight.
Reviewing yourself, little king. Hngh. Vain. ...Also wise. A boss who forgets their abilities dies confused.
Adventurer Intel. Names, classes, threat — the guild's roster, laid bare for you. Study your prey.
This is who is coming to kill you, little king. Read every line. A surprise hero is a dead boss.
Know the enemy. Hngh — the ones who skip this page are the ones I end up burying.
The intel sheet. Some of these heroes are jokes. Some are not. Learn which is which BEFORE the doors open.
Their whole invasion, itemised. Cross-reference it with your maze. That is how a keeper earns the title.
Pre-reading the heroes. Good instinct. I almost did not have to nag you. Almost.
Adventurer Intel. The list of everyone coming to kill you. Read every name — a surprise hero is a dead boss.
Studying the prey before the hunt, little king. Good. Hngh — the keepers who skip this bury their bosses.
Your minion roster. Every loyal little horror on the payroll. Treat them well — they die for you gladly.
The muster list. Hngh. A thin roster is a thin wall between a hero and your throat. Fill it.
Reviewing the troops, little king. Know their strengths. Place them where those strengths bite.
Your standing army. They do not complain, they do not flee, they just hold the line. Be worthy of that.
The roster. Some of these are veterans now — blooded, levelled. Guard them like the assets they are.
Counting heads. Good. An army you do not look at is an army you are about to lose.
Your roster. Every loyal horror on the payroll. Know their strengths — then place them where it bites.
Counting the troops, little king. Good. An army you never look at is an army you are about to waste.
The Dungeon Log. Every kill, every loss, every cruelty — the dungeon keeps receipts. So do I.
The record of the run. Hngh. Read your mistakes here, little king, so you stop repeating them.
The log does not flatter and it does not lie. That is why I like it. Learn from it.
Everything that has happened, written in order. A good keeper studies the past. A great boss does too.
The chronicle of your reign so far. Some entries to be proud of. Some... we do not speak of. Read anyway.
Combing the log. Good. The dungeon remembers everything — might as well make use of its memory.
The Dungeon Log. Every kill and loss, in order. The dungeon keeps receipts — read them and learn.
The wave is counted. Kills, gold, losses — the dungeon's honest little report card.
Tally time, little king. Hngh. Decent numbers. Decent is not done — read what bled.
The post-wave ledger. Learn where the maze leaked, then go and stitch it shut tonight.
The day, summarised. Gold up, heroes down. That is the shape we want. Keep that shape.
Read it properly. Every number here is the dungeon telling you what to build next.
The wave's tally. Gold, kills, losses. Hngh. Read where the maze leaked, then stitch it shut tonight.
Level-up. New power on the table. Choose like it matters — because it does.
The dungeon offers you growth, little king. Pick the cruel option. There is always a cruel option.
Spend this well. Hngh — a wasted level-up is a hero you let live for nothing.
You ascend. Choose the upgrade that wins fights, not the one that looks pretty on the dossier.
More power for the throne. Take it, wield it, and make me glad I stayed.
Level-up. New power on the table. Pick the cruel option — there is always a cruel option, little king.
The final tally. The whole reign in numbers. ...You did not embarrass us. Mostly.
So it ends here. Read it, little king — every line is a lesson for the next throne.
The run's epitaph. Hngh. Not the worst I have served under. Come back and beat it.
Game over. The dungeon falls quiet and I... I will wait. I always wait. Rise again.
The reckoning. Look at what you built before they took it. That much, at least, frightened them.
The final tally. The whole reign in cold numbers. Read it — every line is a lesson for the next throne.
The whole reign, ended and counted. Hngh. ...It was not nothing, little king. We were not nothing. Read it, then rise.
The numbers of a whole reign, ended. Hngh. ...Do not mind me, little king. Even a war-sergeant gets a moment when the throne falls. Just one.
The treasury is nearly bare. Kill more heroes or sell something you regret — but get gold flowing.
We are short on coin, little king. A keeper cannot build with empty hands. Fix that first.
You are HOARDING. All that gold and nowhere to put it. Spend it on cruelty before I spend it for you.
The treasury groans, little king. A fat purse never killed a hero. Buy something with teeth.
So much gold, just sitting there glinting. Greedy. ...I respect the greed. Now spend it.
Not one trap in the whole dungeon. A maze with no bite is just a long, polite hallway.
No traps, little king? Then the heroes simply stroll to your throne. Build some cruelty into the floor.
You have no Trap Factory — so no traps at all. Build one. Let the dungeon kill for you while you watch.
No Barracks? Then no roster minions — nothing standing between a hero and your neck. Fix that tonight.
Build a Library of Whispers, little king. Knowing the next wave before it lands is worth its weight in corpses.
No Treasury. A little gold each dawn adds up — and a keeper hates leaving coin on the table.
A Guard Post would serve you well — minions that hunt instead of standing around looking grim.
Your dungeon is thin on monsters. Empty corridors do not frighten anyone. Hire muscle.
So few minions, little king. The maze is mostly hallway. Fill it with things that bite.
Look at your swarm. A true tyrant of teeth. Hngh — I approve. Keep them fed and bodies stay between you and death.
Such a host of monsters, little king. You build wide and hungry. Good. That is how a dungeon should feel.
A trap-lover, are we? Every corridor a held breath. I do enjoy your taste in cruelty.
So many traps, little king. You let the maze do the murdering. Efficient. Wicked. Continue.
Not a single locked door. A Door Lock funnels heroes exactly where you want them dying.
No locked doors, little king — the heroes wander as they please. Take that freedom from them.
They know us too well. Exposure is climbing. Move the rooms they have mapped — make the maze a stranger again.
The guild has half the dungeon memorised, little king. Reshape it. A solved maze is a dead boss.
The dungeon is still small. A short maze is a quick death — yours. Build it longer, build it meaner.
What a sprawl you have built. The heroes will be old men by the time they reach the throne. I like that.
Early days yet, little king. Lay foundations — a Barracks, a Trap Factory — before you get fancy.
A thought — line your corridors with traps and let the heroes bleed before a single minion lifts a claw.
Place your fiercest minions DEEP, little king. Let the maze tire the heroes out before the real teeth.
Lock a door beside a kill-room. A trapped, cornered hero is a generous hero. They give and give.
Cluster minions of a kind — many archetypes reward a pack. A lone monster is just a snack.
Spend boss XP the moment you have it, little king. A stronger throne is a throne that keeps you on it.
Funnel them — one cruel path is far easier to defend than ten polite ones. Geometry is a weapon.
Guard your Barracks and your Trap Factory. Lose those and the dungeon stops being able to fight back.
Watchtowers and Guard Posts near the entrances, little king. Meet the heroes early. Meet them hard.
A thought — depth wins dungeons. Your fiercest horrors belong DEEP, where the heroes are already bleeding.
Build the maze so every hero feels the throne GLARING at them from the dark. Make them afraid before a single trap springs.
The party is BICKERING. Personalities clashing in the corridors. Their discord is your free damage.
Hngh. Listen to them squabble. A divided party dies divided, little king. Let them.
The heroes turned on each other's nerves. Beautiful. We did not even have to lift a claw.
Their teamwork is unravelling. Internal rot — the cheapest weapon in the dungeon. Savour it.
A clash of egos in MY corridors. The guild really should screen for this. Their loss, our gain.
They are fighting the dungeon AND each other now. Hngh. This is going well, little king.
The mighty heroes, squabbling like market traders. Hngh. The kingdom's finest, little king. Truly. Pathetic.
Day {day}. We have lasted this long, and the kingdom is still failing to kill you. Keep failing them.
Day {day}. Hngh. A lot of dead heroes behind us, little king. A lot more ahead. Onward.
{day} days on the throne. That is not luck anymore. That is you and me, doing the work.
Day {day}. The dungeon is older, meaner, and so are you. The guild should be worried. They are not. Yet.
We have reached day {day}, little king. Most reigns I have kept did not. Do not get comfortable.
Day {day}. Long enough that the heroes tell stories about this place. Make the stories worse.
Day {day}, and the throne still stands. Hngh. I have kept worse bosses for less. You have my blade, little king — still, and gladly.
Day {day}, little king. We have come a long way, you and I. Hngh. ...That crack in my scowl? Leave it. I am allowed it today.
This is your last life. One more death and the reign is over. I am not ready for that. Neither are you.
Final life, little king. Hngh. No more mistakes. None. I mean it — guard yourself.
One life left. The throne has never been this fragile. Build like the dungeon's life depends on it. It does.
Last chance. If they take you now, they take everything. So do not let them. Simple as that.
We are down to the final life. I have served bosses to this point and lost them. Not you. NOT you.
The final life, little king. Hngh. A keeper is not supposed to feel dread. ...I feel it. Guard yourself like never before.
One life left, little king. Hngh. Then I stand at the door and I do not move. Whatever comes, it goes through me first.
Your last life. Hngh. I have lost bosses at this exact moment, little king, and I — ...no. Not you. I am not burying you. Hold the line.
One life. ONE, little king. I will say it plain — this frightens me, and a keeper does not frighten easy. Build like everything depends on it.
That is {count} days running, with the WHOLE party wiped out. Hngh. The kingdom is running out of heroes.
{count} clean sweeps in a row, little king. The guild must dread the sight of this place. Good.
A {count}-day wipe streak. I did not think you had this in you. I am pleased to be wrong.
{count} days, not one survivor. The dungeon is eating well, little king. Keep its plate full.
{count} total wipes running. Hngh. This is what we are supposed to look like. Do not stop.
A streak of {count}. The heroes who march in now are basically delivering themselves. Generous of them.
{count} days, every party wiped. Hngh. I keep waiting for you to slip and you simply... do not. Astonishing.
{minionName} has {count} kills now. That is no mere minion — that is a veteran. Guard it.
{count} heroes dead by one minion's hand. Hngh. {minionName} is earning its place, little king.
{minionName} hit {count} kills. The thing has a reputation now. Reputations get hunted — keep it safe.
{count} kills for {minionName}. Loyalty and a body count. That is my favourite kind of minion.
Look at {minionName} — {count} heroes in the dirt. Promote it in your heart, little king. It earned it.
{minionName} reaches {count} kills. Hngh. Some minions just stand there. This one WORKS. Reward it with heroes.
{count} kills for {minionName}. Hngh. ...I will own it, little king — watching one of ours become a legend puts a rare warmth in this old keeper.
The ground itself turns on them. Feel that? That is the dungeon taking your side.
An earthquake at your command, little king. The heroes do not get to fight the FLOOR. Magnificent.
You shook the world and the heroes fell down in it. Hngh. Show-off. ...Do it again.
A whole room of heroes, floored at once. Hngh. ...I forget, sometimes, what you can DO, little king.
You fed a minion into the dark for power. Cold. Effective. It died useful — that is more than most get.
A sacrifice, little king. One of ours, spent for strength. Hngh. ...Make its death worth the price.
Power bought with a loyal life. I will not pretend I love it — but I will not pretend it does not work.
You tore a gate to somewhere worse. Whatever crawls through it answers to you now. Terrifying. Good.
A hellgate, little king. The heroes came expecting a dungeon. They got a doorway to the abyss.
Reinforcements from the pit itself. Hngh. I do love when the dungeon cheats in your favour.
Stone. You turned a hero to stone mid-stride. They wanted a statue of themselves — granted.
Petrified, little king. A hero frozen solid is a hero who has stopped being a problem. Lovely.
Look at it — stuck, grey, useless. Hngh. The kingdom can collect it later. With a chisel.
You charmed one of them. Now a hero fights for the throne. Their friends will HATE that.
A turned hero, little king. Watching the guild's own blade swing for us — that never gets old.
Charmed and obedient. Hngh. Use them up — a borrowed hero does not need to survive the day.
You scatter into a bat-swarm and charm one of them mid-march, little king. Hngh. Theatrical. ...It works, so I will allow it.
Your charmed hero turns on their own party — and keeps swinging until an ally lies dead. Borrowed muscle. Spend it.
Bat-form, a kiss, a traitor in their ranks. I do not do seduction. But a guild gutting itself? That I respect.
The pack is loose. Nothing in the maze runs faster than a thing that hunts. Let them run it down.
A hunting pack at your word, little king. The heroes are prey now. They have not realised yet.
Hngh. Listen to them coordinate. THAT is how you swarm a hero. Teeth from every angle.
Spores in the air. The heroes are breathing your dungeon in. It does not agree with them. Heh.
A spore cloud, little king. Invisible, patient, lethal. Cruelty you cannot even see coming. Perfect.
They are choking on the dungeon itself. Hngh. The air on your side — I do enjoy that.
You put fear into them. A haunted hero swings at shadows. Their courage is rotting from inside.
The haunt takes hold, little king. Watch a brave fool come apart with nothing to fight. Beautiful.
Dread in the corridors. Hngh. The maze does not even need to touch them — their own minds finish the job.
Look at the hero — frozen mid-stride, eyes wide, useless. Fear stops a brave fool dead, little king. Beautiful.
The coin came up FOR us. The treasury swells. Hngh — I would not have risked it, but I will spend it.
You gambled and you won, little king. Reckless. Lucky. ...Do not make a habit of needing luck.
The bet paid off. Gold for nothing. I still think it was foolish. I still think we should count it. Now.
Fortune blinked your way. Take the winnings, little king, and pretend you planned it. I will not tell.
It came up FOR us? Hngh — I had braced for ruin, little king, not a fat purse. Count it before the luck changes its mind.
The coin betrayed you. Gold, gone. THIS is why I do not gamble — the dungeon is risk enough.
You lost the bet, little king. Hngh. I would roast you for it, but you already feel the sting. Mostly.
Coin down the drain. A keeper hates watching gold die for nothing. Earn it back the honest way — with corpses.
The gamble failed. Of course it did, little king — that is what gambles mostly do. Lesson learned? ...Doubt it.
You bet the treasury and the coin laughed at you. Hngh. So do I, little king — quietly, loyally, but I do.

specifics.note: Keyed bespoke-line banks — specifics[domain][id]. NpcDirector prefers a bespoke line when one exists for an entity, else falls back to the generic category. Authored in batches; an absent id is safe.

beholder.t: A Beholder on the throne. All those eyes, and every one of them is going to watch a hero die. I can work with that.
Your gaze turns flesh to stone. Hngh. Useful. Just never aim it at the help, little king.
Anti-magic hangs in your halls — the casters will choke on it. Good. I have always hated casters.
So many eyes and not one of them watching the corridors properly. Relax — that is what I am for.

demon.t: A Demon Lord on the throne. NOW we are talking. The Hellgate spits out free imps — cheap fuel, endless fuel.
You can burn one of your own to gut a hero. Cold. Efficient. Hngh — I respect a boss who spends bodies well.
Imps, imps, endless imps. A dungeon that restocks itself, little king. This is my kind of reign.
A Demon at the heart of the maze. The kingdom should have stayed home. Let us teach them that lesson.

myconid.t: A Myconid. Your dungeon does not fight the heroes so much as... digest them. Patiently. I can be patient.
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming into Vinekin. Your dungeon GARDENS, little king. Grim. Effective.
Rot and patience — your whole strategy. Hngh. Not flashy. But the heroes die just as dead.
Every corpse a seed. The maze grows its own army. Lazy of it. ...I mean that as a compliment.

wraith.t: A Wraith on the throne. You do not need swords — you need them afraid. And they will be. I will see to it.
Fear does the killing for you, little king. At full terror the heroes simply stop. Drop. Done. Elegant.
Every corpse spawns a Ghost — the dungeon haunts itself for free. Hngh. Frugal AND terrifying. I like it.
Terror. No mess, no upkeep, no mercy. The heroes defeat themselves. We just open the door.
A Wraith. Hngh. You do not chase the heroes — you LOOM, and they break. The whole kingdom will learn to dread the dark of you.

gnoll.t: A Gnoll Alpha. The pack grows, the pack feeds, the pack snowballs — by dusk they are monsters. I love a snowball.
Every kill makes every gnoll angrier, little king. Bloodlust with interest. Keep them killing.
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn — the kingdom funds your army without knowing it. Heh. Fools.
Your pack hunts as one mind. A hive of teeth. Hngh. Point it at the heroes and step back.

golem.t: An Earth Golem. Every room you place makes YOU tougher. You literally ARE the dungeon. Build greedily.
Build wide and you build a fortress of stone bones, little king. The bigger the maze, the bigger you.
One Earthquake a day and a whole room of heroes just... folds. Hngh. I will never tire of watching that.
Slow, vast, unkillable. A boss after my own cold heart. Keep building — every room is armour.

lich.t: An Elder Lich. A hidden heart, a spare life, an army that recruits itself from the dead. Sneaky. I approve.
Every hero you kill rises again at dawn — for YOU. The guild keeps mailing you soldiers, little king. Idiots.
Hide the phylactery well. A fourth life is a luxury, and luxuries get stolen if you are careless.
Death is a door you walk both ways. The heroes only ever learn the one direction. Hngh. Use that.

lizardman.t: A Serpent Captain. Your minions are invisible until they strike — a free ambush every time. Beautiful.
Venom that stacks and stacks. The heroes do not die in your dungeon, little king — they die on the walk home.
Unseen and poisonous. Hngh. The honest way to fight — which is to say, not honest at all.
They cannot route around what they cannot see. Camouflage is just cheating politely. Cheat away.
A Serpent Captain. Hngh. Let the heroes feel hunted by something they cannot even SEE. Fear in the dark is the cruellest blade.

orc.t: An Orc Veteran. Every kill makes an orc permanently stronger — your soldiers never stop climbing. NOW we are talking.
Pack the orcs into one room, little king — Warband makes each fiercer for every brother beside it.
A veteran orc late in a run hits like a small, furious army. Patience pays in blood. I have plenty of patience.
Blood for blood, strength forever. Crude. Brutal. Honest. Hngh — this is the boss I would have picked.

vampire.t: A Vampire Sovereign. You charm a hero each day and they betray their own. Poetic cruelty. I am almost moved.
Blood Tax — every hit your minions land feeds YOU, little king. The dungeon is one long red meal.
You are the fighter; the minions are the cutlery. Hngh. Elegant. Just do not forget to actually fight.
A creature of the night on the throne. Refined taste in cruelty. We will get along — provided you keep winning.

succubus.t: A Succubus Queen. All charm and bat-swarms. Hngh — not my style. But the heroes die just the same, so. Fine.
You scatter into bats and charm a hero mid-march, little king. Flashy. ...Effective, I will grant you. Effective.
Your charmed thrall turns on their own until they kill a friend. Cruel. Sneaky. I did not expect to enjoy this. I do.
Charm the heroes, let them gut each other. Lazy. Vicious. ...You know what? It works. Carry on.

knight.t: A knight — all plate, no imagination. Slow to kill, slower to think. Hngh. Almost too easy.
