Here comes a knight, little king. They march straight at the throne. Predictable. Punishable.
A knight. A thick shell — pile on enough teeth and the shell cracks like any other. Pile on the teeth.
Armour, a big sword, a bigger ego. The kingdom's favourite, the dungeon's favourite snack. Feed them to it.
A knight. Hngh. All that polished steel just to clank slowly toward a death it cannot dodge. Adorable.

rogue.t: A rogue slips in. Watch this one — picks locks, turns invisible, stabs from behind. No honour. Effective.
A rogue, little king. Your locked doors mean nothing to those clever little fingers. Plan around it.
A rogue. They go for the treasure, not the throne. Greedy. Hngh — greed is a leash. Use it.
A rogue in the halls. Fights dirty, runs faster. ...I respect dirty. Now kill it anyway.

mage.t: A mage. Glass and fury — kill it FAST, before the spells start flying. Do not let it breathe.
Here comes a mage — all that power crammed into something that dies if you sneeze on it. So sneeze on it.
A mage, little king. An anti-magic room would ruin its whole afternoon. Just a thought. A good one.
A mage. Squishy, dangerous, far too pleased with itself. Pop it quick, and pop it first.

cleric.t: A cleric. It will heal the others — kill the cleric FIRST, and the whole party rots faster.
Here comes a cleric, little king. Holy light and a sour attitude toward the dead. Tiresome. End it early.
A cleric. Cut the healer and watch the party's hope drain out with it. Always the healer first. Always.
A cleric walks in, praying. Hngh. The dungeon does not take requests. Prove it to them.
A cleric, mumbling to its god, little king. Hngh. No god has ever once answered a prayer in MY corridors.

necromancer.t: A necromancer. The CHEEK of it — it will raise YOUR dead minions and turn them against you.
Here comes a necromancer, little king. A corpse-thief. Do not let it have ours. Guard the fallen.
A necromancer. It plays with death. Hngh — adorable. We INVENTED playing with death. Show it the masters.
A necromancer in the halls. Kill it before it makes traitors of your own soldiers. Fast.

ranger.t: A ranger. It peppers your minions from range and disarms your traps. Tiresome. Close the distance.
Here comes a ranger, little king. Arrows and trap-sense. Get it into a corridor where the bow is useless.
A ranger. It loves that bow. Hngh. Take away its range and you take away its courage. Crowd it.
A ranger, eyes on every trap. Your hidden teeth may not stay hidden. Have a backup that bites.

twitch_streamer.t: A twitch streamer. Thousands watching. Hngh. Then let us put on a funeral worth the view count.
A streamer, little king. Its chat decides its moves — half genius, half suicidal. Pure chaos. Exploit the suicidal half.
Here comes a streamer. Kill it well and the clip spreads — a bigger crowd marches in tomorrow. More to bury.
A streamer in the dungeon. Wave at the little viewers. They came to watch you be a monster. Oblige them.

beast_master.t: A beast master. It will TAME one of your minions and turn it on you. The audacity. Watch the lone ones.
Here comes a beast master, little king. Mono-type rooms are a liability now — give it no easy pet.
A beast master. It steals a monster's loyalty. Hngh — as if monsters had loyalty to spare. Ours had better.
A beast master, all whistles and treats. Your minions had best not charm so easily. Mine never would.

barbarian.t: A barbarian. The more you hurt it, the harder it hits. Do NOT do this one slowly. Burst it down.
Here comes a barbarian, little king. Immune to fear, immune to fleeing. No tricks — just overwhelming force.
A barbarian. All rage, no retreat. Wound it halfway and you have only made it worse. Finish it or do not start.
A barbarian in the halls. Subtlety is wasted on this one. Meet the brute with a bigger brute.

monk.t: A monk. Fast, unarmoured, and it DODGES — traps included. Slippery little saint. Crowd it.
Here comes a monk, little king. Half your minions' armour means nothing to those fists. Out-number it.
A monk. It counters trap-heavy halls — wide nasty hazards beat precise ones here. Adjust.
A monk glides in — hard to hit, hard to trap. Hngh. Even a monk cannot dodge everything at once. Swarm it.

bard.t: A bard. While it lives the whole party hits harder and moves faster. Kill the bard. I cannot stress this enough.
Here comes a bard, little king — a walking buff. Silence the song and the party falls apart.
A bard. Charming, useless alone, dreadful in a pack. Make it the priority. The music dies first.
A bard struts in, playing. Hngh. The dungeon is no patron of the arts. Cut the performance short.

cartographer_scholar.t: A cartographer-scholar. It did not come for you — it came to MAP us. Worse than a sword. Stop it leaving.
A scholar with spectacles and a map, little king. Every room it finishes, it sells to the next wave. Bury it.
A cartographer. Harmless in a fight, catastrophic if it walks out. Do NOT let this one walk out.
Here comes a scholar — all curiosity, no blade. Hngh. Curiosity has killed a great many of them. Add one more.

cosplay_adventurer.t: A costumed one — dressed up as a monster. It will mostly ignore your minions. Annoying loophole.
A cosplayer, little king. An adorable little outfit. Hngh. It will make an adorable little corpse.
Here comes one in monster fancy-dress. It plays nice with minions — so the TRAPS must do the talking.
A cosplay adventurer. It came to play pretend. The dungeon, plays for keeps. Remind it.
Look at it — a hero dressed as a monster. Hngh. Playing at being us. It will die a far worse actor than a corpse.

guild_raid.t: A Guild Raid. Twice the heroes tomorrow — the local Guild finally noticed us. Build deep.
The Guild is sending a crowd, little king. More bodies for the maze, more gold for the vault. I am not worried.
Double the wave incoming. Hngh. Steady pressure, not one surge — but reinforce anyway. Do not be lazy.

legendary_speedrunner.t: A legendary speedrunner — chasing a record through OUR halls. The sheer nerve of it.
This one ignores everything and bolts for the throne, little king. Twice the speed. Wall the path. Now.
Kill the record-chaser and your boss drinks a feast of XP. Get in its way — make it lose, slowly.

dungeon_pestilence.t: Pestilence. Your minions wake at half health tomorrow — and a hero's touch spreads the rot. Plan around sick soldiers.
A plague in the pit, little king. Sickly minions — but any hero who melees them catches the Blight. Cruel symmetry. I like it.
Your soldiers are ill. Place a few extra — half a minion is still half a set of teeth.

cartographers_convention.t: Cartographers. They will not fight — they came to MAP us and sell it. Do NOT let them leave.
A convention of scholars, little king. Every room they finish raises its infamy forever. Kill them in the corridors.
Spectacled little map-makers. Hngh. Harmless in a brawl, catastrophic out the door. Hunt every one down.

blood_moon_eclipse.t: A Blood Moon. Your minions hit twice as hard tomorrow — and bleed twice as fast. Glorious, dangerous risk.
The eclipse empowers the dark, little king. Double damage given AND taken. Win fast or do not win at all.
Blood Moon — no gold from the dying tomorrow. Hngh. Fight for the joy of the carnage. There are worse reasons.

negotiation_day.t: A diplomat — no swords today. Pay tribute for a free day, or refuse and fatten the next wave. Your call.
The Guild sends a negotiator, little king. Greed says keep the gold; caution says buy the quiet. Choose well.
No combat today — only a bargain. Hngh. Talking instead of killing. Strange day. Decide quickly.

rival_dungeon.t: A rival dungeon empties into ours — monsters, not heroes, and a boss bringing up the rear. Insolent.
Another dungeon's master wants YOUR throne, little king. Kill their boss for a feast of gold and XP. Send a message.
Rival monsters in the halls. Hngh. Let them and our minions sort it out — then we take the spoils.

twitch_con.t: Twitch Con. Tomorrow every hero is a streamer. Pure, glorious, chat-driven chaos. Exploit it.
A dungeon full of streamers, little king. Thousands of viewers. Make it a massacre worth clipping.
All streamers tomorrow — and killing them brings no escalation. A free day of carnage. Hngh. Enjoy it.

dark_deal.t: A demon at the door tonight — a free pact for half your health tomorrow. Tempting. Bloody. Your kind of choice.
The Dark Deal, little king. Click the demon and a pact is yours, gratis — at a steep price. I would hear it out.
A free bargain offered. Refuse and the demon just leaves. Hngh. At least LISTEN. Power is power.

cosplay_contest.t: A Cosplay Contest. Heroes in monster fancy-dress — they will mostly ignore your minions. Annoying loophole.
Costumed adventurers tomorrow, little king. Your minions get snubbed, so the TRAPS must do the killing.
Adventurers playing dress-up. Hngh. Adorable. The dungeon will undress them the hard way.

loot_goblin_heist.t: Loot Goblins — they spawn in the boss room and BOLT for the exit. Every escapee robs your treasury. MOVE.
A goblin heist, little king. They will not fight — only run, fat with your gold. Cut them down before the door.
Goblins in the hoard! Each one killed pays a fortune; each one fled steals one. Do not stand there — hunt.

tax_season.t: Tax Season. The Guild skims a fifth of the treasury — but every kill pays double. Kill enough to make it back.
The taxman cometh, little king. A bite out of the gold today, doubled coin on every corpse. Kill generously.
They tax the treasury and call it law. Hngh. We take it back — one dead hero at a time.

patrons_blessing.t: A Patron's Blessing. Double boss XP from every kill tomorrow. A pure, lovely upside. Do not waste it.
The dark smiles on you today, little king. Every kill feeds your ascension twice over. Hunt well.
Doubled XP and no catch. Hngh. Suspicious. ...Take it anyway. We do not look gift-curses in the mouth.

memory_plague.t: Memory Plague — the Guild's intel on us is WIPED. The next wave walks in blind. Heh. Delicious.
Their maps, their notes, their warnings — gone, little king. A fresh, ignorant wave. The maze will eat well.
The heroes forgot everything they learned. Teach them new lessons. Permanent ones, this time.

gamblers_coin.t: The Gambler's Coin. Wager the treasury — heads it doubles, tails it halves. Hngh. I would not. You will.
A coin flip for all your gold, little king. Reckless. ...If you must gamble, gamble decisively. Do not dither.
Double or nothing on the whole treasury. A keeper hates watching gold ride on a coin. Hates it. Choose.

dense_fog.t: Dense Fog tomorrow. Whatever the heroes learn registers as mere rumour. Exposure barely stirs. Good cover.
A grey fog over everything, little king. Their intel will not stick. The maze stays a mystery a while longer.
Fog in the halls. Hngh. The heroes squint and guess. Guessing, is how the careless ones die.

miasma.t: Creeping Miasma. A green rot bleeds EVERYTHING — invaders, minions, and you. Win quick or the dungeon eats us too.
The miasma picks no sides, little king. It whittles the heroes down — and gnaws at you. Do not linger.
A poison haze fills the dungeon. Cruel to them, unkind to us. Hngh. Risk and reward. Mostly risk.

tremors.t: Tremors tomorrow. The dungeon shudders — a random room takes a hit every few breaths. The maze itself fights.
The earth will not sit still, little king. Quakes lethal to invaders, merely rude to your minions. Use it.
Shaking halls. Hngh. The dungeon has opinions today. Let it voice them — into the heroes.

arcane_storm.t: An Arcane Storm. The heroes' spellcasters fire relentlessly tomorrow — cooldowns gutted. Kill the casters FIRST.
Magic crackles in the air, little king; every caster gets greedy with spells. Brace the front line.
A storm of arcane. Your boss powers are untouched, thankfully — but their mages will not be. End them fast.

bounty_hunters.t: Bounty Hunters — a pack of five, all hunting your strongest minion. Fame has a price. Pay it in their blood.
Five hunters, one target: your best minion, little king. Wall them off it, or lose your finest soldier.
The Guild put coin on our champion's head. Hngh. Make their contract very, very unprofitable.

zombie_horde.t: A Zombie Horde — fourteen slow, witless, fearless dead, shambling for the throne. Grind them in a corridor.
Undead by the dozen, little king. Weak — but they NEVER flee and never stop. Build a kill-corridor for them.
A shamble of corpses tomorrow. The maze loves a slow target. So do the traps. Let them feast.

infamy_spike.t: An Infamy Spike. Tomorrow's wave is bigger AND every hero is upgraded to Hero grade. Build like you mean it.
Our reputation precedes us, little king — a swollen wave of gold-ringed elites. This is the cost of being feared.
Fame draws the strong. A whole wave of Heroes. Hngh. Flattering. Now go and earn the flattery — reinforce.

black_market.t: A Black Market opens tonight. Fifty gold for a free minion, delivered to your halls. That is a steal — take it.
The Black Market beckons, little king. Cheap muscle, if you have the coin. I would take the deal.
Fifty gold, one free wretch. Hngh. The dark runs a tidy little shop. Spend — an extra body is an extra wall.

mercenary_contract.t: A Mercenary Contract. A hundred and twenty gold buys an elite — but it fights only three days. Expensive courage.
Hired muscle, little king. Doubled stats, three days, and if it dies in battle it dies for GOOD. Weigh it.
An elite for rent. Pricey, temporary, deadly. Hngh — the kind of bargain I respect. Your call.

cursed_relic.t: A Cursed Relic offered tonight. Claim it for daily gold — but every wave doubles while it sits. Greed with teeth.
The relic glows wrong, little king. Free coin, doubled heroes. Sell it later to lift the curse. Tempting. Dangerous.
Claim the cursed chest or banish it. Greed pays well — and bites harder. Hngh. Your favourite kind of choice.

the_saboteur.t: The Saboteur — one masked ghost, untouchable, here to disarm EVERY trap. Insufferable little vandal.
A lone saboteur tomorrow, little king. Your minions cannot touch it; your traps are dead for the day. Lean on bodies.
An untouchable vandal, switching off our teeth one by one. Hngh. The maze will be all muscle tomorrow. Bring muscle.

entry_hall.t: An Entry Hall. The heroes pour in through these — every door is a front you must defend. Mind them all.
Another way in, little king. The kingdom forces these on you as you grow. More doors, more fires for me to put out.
An entrance placed. Funnel it into a kill-zone — make their very first step their worst one.

starter_corridor.t: A corridor. No teeth of its own — but a corridor is where you HANG the teeth. Line it with cruelty.
Connective tissue, little king. Dull alone — lethal once you pack it with traps and patrolling minions.
A corridor placed. Hngh. Every cell of this dungeon should hurt. Do not waste this one on mere walking.

starter_barracks.t: A Barracks — ten more roster slots, your only source of minions that truly roam and patrol. Essential.
More Barracks, more pack, little king. Without one you have no roster minions at all. Build it early.
A Barracks. The beating heart of your army. Place it deep and guard it like your life — because it is.

starter_guard_post.t: A Guard Post. Minions here hunt into any door-connected room — a wandering ambush. I like aggression.
A Guard Post placed, little king. Its minions leave to kill, then come home. Hngh. Proper soldiers.
Guard Post. Put it where its reach covers a corridor the heroes must walk. Then let it work.

crypt.t: A Crypt. Four Risen Bones, refilled each night, free of the roster cap. Cheap, endless, expendable dead.
A Crypt placed, little king. Garrison skeletons — room-bound but tireless. The dungeon's discount army.
The Crypt gives without taking a roster slot. Hngh. Something for almost nothing. I approve.

trap_factory.t: A Trap Factory — five trap slots, and the ONLY way to deploy traps at all. Build this or have no traps. Simple.
The forge of cruelty, little king. Without a Trap Factory your halls have no hidden bite. Place it.
A Trap Factory placed. Now the maze can lie in wait. Hngh — my favourite kind of architecture.

treasury.t: A Treasury. A daily gold stipend — and bait that lures bigger, richer waves. Risk and reward. Guard it.
A Treasury placed, little king. Coin every dawn, plus chests the greedy will lunge for. Watch them well.
Glittering bait. It draws the raid groups — more XP, more danger. Hngh. Be ready for what the gold attracts.

armory.t: An Armory. Minions in door-connected rooms swing harder while it stands. Sharpen the front line.
An Armory placed, little king. A quiet buff to every neighbour. Cluster your killers around it.
More attack for the rooms beside it. Hngh. Build it where your fiercest minions wait. Make them deadlier.

library_of_whispers.t: A Library of Whispers. It reads the next party for you — names, classes, more as it grows. Knowledge is a blade.
The Library, little king. Knowledge of THEM before they ever arrive. Forewarned is forearmed. Use it.
A Library placed. Now we spy on the Guild's homework. Hngh. Delightfully unfair. Just how I like it.

watchtower.t: A Watchtower. Minions in connected rooms land a free first strike when heroes enter. The first blow is everything.
A Watchtower placed, little king. A free opening hit — and the first hit often decides the rest.
First strike for the neighbours. Speedrunners HATE this. Hngh. So build more of them.

wandering_gate.t: A Wandering Gate. Step through and the maze SPITS you somewhere else — maybe the boss room. Chaos. I like chaos.
A Wandering Gate placed, little king. It scatters their careful little plans across the floor. Beautiful.
The Gate teleports the unwary. A small chance it drops them right at the throne. Hngh. A long shot worth taking.

veil_of_forgetting.t: A Veil of Forgetting. Each night it wipes the heroes' memory of every room beside it. Forgetting is a weapon.
The Veil placed, little king. Connected rooms become mysteries again, every dawn. Un-teach them.
It un-teaches the heroes. Ring your nastiest rooms with it and they walk in blind, forever. Hngh. Cruel. Good.

catacombs.t: Catacombs. A hero who dies here RISES as a Revenant for you. The dead change sides. Poetic, that.
The Catacombs placed, little king. Their fallen become your garrison. Profitable AND insulting to the Guild.
Where heroes die here, soldiers stand up. The dungeon recruits at the funeral. Hngh. Efficient.

mimic_vault.t: A Mimic Vault. It LOOKS like a treasury — but the chests bite back. Greed walks right in. I love watching it.
The Mimic Vault placed, little king. Disguised teeth wearing a treasure's smile. The maze's finest lie.
Bait that is also the trap. The greedy ones never learn. Hngh — and I adore that about them.

hall_of_trials.t: A Hall of Trials. A random Tier-2 evolved minion stands guard each night. Free and fierce. Take the gift.
The Hall of Trials placed, little king. A surprise champion every dawn — the heroes never know what waits.
A free evolved minion, refreshed nightly. Hngh. The dungeon spoils you. Do not get soft about it.

wishing_well.t: A Wishing Well. A coin flip on entry — a hero is blessed, or MARKED for extra death. I like those odds.
The Wishing Well placed, little king. Heads they thrive; tails they take far more from your minions. Cruel luck.
A well of wishes. Half come true horribly. Place it on a path they cannot avoid. Make them wish.

false_exit.t: A False Exit. Fleeing heroes bolt for it — and it spits them back into the maze. Hngh. Cruel comedy. My favourite genre.
The False Exit placed, little king. A door marked SAFETY that leads anywhere but. The runners earned it.
Hope, then nothing. A fleeing hero trades the exit for a random room. Despair grows so very nicely here.

hall_of_madness.t: A Hall of Madness. Heroes inside may turn on each OTHER. Let them do your killing for you. Lazy. Brilliant.
The Hall of Madness placed, little king. Madness is contagious — and free. Watch the party eat itself.
They walk in a party and leave a brawl. Or do not leave at all. Hngh. Delicious work.

throne_room.t: A Throne Room — it spawns a Mini-Boss that scales with you, refreshed each night. No small thing. Place it well.
The Throne Room placed, little king. A garrison champion, room-bound but mighty. No other minion may share it.
A lesser throne for a greater guard. The Mini-Boss grows as you do. Hngh. Put it on the path to YOU.

sanctum.t: A Sanctum — the capstone. Your boss regenerates between fights, and connected minions too. We have come far.
The Sanctum placed, little king. Healing for the throne and its neighbours. The dungeon, at last, tends its own.
Your final unlock. A room that mends what the heroes break. Hngh. ...Well built. I mean that.

skeleton.t: A skeleton — cheap, brittle, endlessly replaceable. The dungeon's bread and butter. Buy plenty.
Another skeleton, little king. They die easy and rise easier. Hngh. Beautifully expendable.
A skeleton placed. Not much alone — but the dead are never alone for long. Stack them.

orc.t: An orc. Brutish, proud, sharpens itself with every kill. Feed it heroes and it just gets worse.
An orc joins the pit, little king. Pack them close — orcs fight fiercer shoulder to shoulder.
An orc placed. All muscle and grudge. Hngh. The heroes will not enjoy this one. Good.

goblin.t: A goblin — small, shrieky, best in numbers. One is a nuisance; ten is an actual problem.
A goblin in the halls, little king. Weak, fast, gleefully spiteful. Cheap chaos. I will take it.
Another goblin. Do not expect heroics — expect a swarm that simply will not shut up.

imp.t: An imp — a quick, vicious scrap of the inferno. It enjoys the work. Hngh. So do I.
An imp placed, little king. Fast and nasty. The heroes always underestimate the little ones.
An imp joins us. Hellish, hyperactive, and on YOUR side for once. Use the speed.

ghost.t: A ghost. It drifts through walls and unnerves the living. Eerie. Effective. I approve of both.
A ghost placed, little king. No door stops it, no hero relaxes near it. Hngh. Lovely.
Another ghost for the halls. The heroes hate fighting what they cannot corner. Lean into that.

slime.t: A slime. It does not look like much — and that, precisely, is the point. Let them underestimate it.
A slime placed, little king. Squishy, patient, underestimated to death. The heroes never learn.
Another slime. Hngh. Charmingly disgusting. Park it where some fool must squelch through.

elder_slime.t: An elder slime — a slime that ate well and grew opinions. Sturdier than it looks. Respect the ooze.
An elder slime placed, little king. Years of digestion made it large and grim. It will hold a line.
Another elder slime. The patient ones, become the dangerous ones. Hngh. I would know.

golem.t: A golem. Slow as a grudge and twice as hard to budge. Park it where heroes MUST pass.
A golem placed, little king. A wall that hits back. The heroes will tire long before it does.
Another golem. Tremendous, tireless, immovable. Hngh. The dungeon's stubborn heart. I like it.

beholder.t: A beholder minion — all eyes, all menace. It strikes from range, so shield it well or lose it.
A beholder placed, little king. Floating spite. Keep something tough between it and the heroes.
Another beholder. It does not need to be close to be cruel. Hngh — my favourite trait in anything.

lich.t: A lich minion. Undead, spiteful, clever — death taught it patience. A dangerous student.
A lich placed, little king. A caster that does not breathe and does not tire. Dreadful little scholar.
Another lich for the halls. The heroes fear the thinking dead most of all. Use that fear.

lizardman.t: A lizardman. Venom on every strike — it need not win the fight, only START the poison. Patient killer.
A lizardman placed, little king. Scaled, toxic, patient. The heroes die slow, and far from here.
Another lizardman. One bite and the clock starts ticking. Hngh. I do love a slow knife.

gnoll.t: A gnoll — the pack instinct runs deep. Cluster them and the snarling math adds up fast.
A gnoll placed, little king. Hungry, vicious, better in numbers. Give it brothers. Many.
Another gnoll. The pack grows. Hngh. The pack always wants to grow. Let it.

demon.t: A demon minion — a scrap of the inferno on the payroll. Cruel by nature, loyal by contract. The best hire.
A demon placed, little king. Hellfire and bad intentions. Hngh. My kind get along just fine.
Another demon for the halls. It enjoys the work — and so will you, watching it.

ent.t: An ent — a walking wall of old wood. Tremendous patience, tremendous health. Park it and forget it.
An ent placed, little king. The heroes will tire of hacking long before it falls. Let them swing.
Another ent. Slow, ancient, immovable. Hngh. The forest holds a grudge. Aim the grudge.

plant.t: A vinekin — rooted, thorny, dreadfully underestimated. Let it grow on someone unfortunate.
A vinekin placed, little king. Quiet until it is not. The heroes never watch the shrubbery.
Another vinekin. Cheap, green, full of thorns. Hngh. The garden bites in this dungeon.

mushroom.t: A mushroom minion. Spores and spite — it poisons the very air around it. Cruelty you cannot see.
A mushroom placed, little king. Keep the heroes breathing near it. Lovely, lingering rot.
Another mushroom. Fungal and patient. Hngh. The dungeon does love a slow poison.

rat.t: A rat. One rat is nothing. A NEST of rats is an entirely different conversation. Build the nest.
A rat placed, little king. Cheap, quick, disposable. Drown the heroes in the small things.
Another rat. Vermin by the handful. Not glorious — but glory never won a corridor. Numbers did.

zombie.t: A zombie. Slow, witless, utterly relentless. It does not tire and it does not stop. ...Bit like me.
A zombie placed, little king. No speed, no cleverness — just an endless, shuffling NO.
Another zombie. The heroes can outrun it. Hngh. What they cannot do is outlast it. Wear them down.

vampire_minion.t: A vampire thrall. It drinks what it strikes. Every blow it lands feeds it — the heroes weaken, it does not.
A thrall placed, little king. Charming, pale, parasitic. Hngh. Put it in a long fight and watch it thrive.
Another vampire thrall. It turns a brawl into a meal. Loyal AND self-sufficient. I like this one.

shooting_arrows.t: Shooting Arrows — wall-mounted, line-of-sight, with a lingering poison. Aim the lane well or waste it.
An arrow trap placed, little king. It fires forever and never breaks. Hngh. The perfect employee.
Shooting Arrows. Small sting, slow poison. The cruelty is in the WAIT. I respect a patient trap.

bomb.t: A Bomb — a few seconds of fuse, then ruin for everything in five tiles. Your minions included. Mind that.
A bomb placed, little king. One use, enormous noise. Put it where a crowd will gather. Then enjoy it.
A Bomb. It does not discriminate — heroes AND your own. Place it boldly, or do not place it at all.

cannon.t: A Cannon — wrought iron that fires down its line forever. Rotate it cruelly. Aim it crueller.
A cannon placed, little king. A cannonball for any hero in its sights. Hngh. It never tires of the work.
A Cannon. Heavy, blunt, endless. Point it down a corridor they cannot avoid and let it speak.

spike_pillar.t: A Spike Pillar. It spits spikes at anything within a tile. Solid, vicious, central. No subtlety — I like that.
A spike pillar placed, little king. A blunt little horror — it bites everything that crowds it.
A Spike Pillar. All puncture, no manners. The heroes will give it a frightened berth. Make berths costly.

dragon_trap.t: A Dragon Trap — wall-mounted, and it breathes FIRE down its line. Heavy, heavy damage. Now THAT is a trap.
A dragon trap placed, little king. The maze just learned to spit flame. They will cook in a corridor.
A Dragon Trap. The nastiest wall in the dungeon. Hngh. Aim it where they cannot dodge.

spike_pit.t: A Spike Pit — hidden until they step on it. Heavy damage, and a chance to simply END a hero outright.
A spike pit placed, little king. Patient, invisible, occasionally lethal in one go. Tuck it deep.
A Spike Pit. They will not see it until they are IN it. Hngh. The maze's quietest killer. I approve.

rotating_blades.t: Rotating Blades — forever spinning, forever hungry for anything in reach. It does not aim. It does not need to.
A blade trap placed, little king. It does not stop, it does not think — it simply cuts. Hngh. Lovely.
Rotating Blades. A wall of whirling teeth. The heroes must thread it or bleed for it. Most bleed.

saw_blade.t: A Saw Blade — it travels its track endlessly, slicing any hero it overlaps. Make them dance.
A saw blade placed, little king. Walkable track, unwalkable timing. Hngh. Cruelty with a metronome.
A Saw Blade. Heavy damage and no collision — it shares the floor and ruins it anyway. Place it mean.

door_lock.t: A Door Lock — it seals the way until a hero finds the key. A chokepoint with manners. Funnel them.
A door lock placed, little king. It pairs with a Key Chest somewhere — guard that chest, or this is theatre.
A Door Lock. Funnel them, frustrate them, make them HUNT for the way through. Hngh. Delicious.

treasure_chest.t: A Treasure Chest. Passive gold each day — and a shiny temptation the greedy cannot resist. Bait that pays.
A chest placed, little king. It pays you nightly and lures the raid groups. Guard it well — or do not, and feast.
A Treasure Chest. Hngh. Bait that earns its keep. The best lies are the profitable ones.

soul_bound_beacon.t: A Soul-Bound Beacon — and its Healing Fountain. A safe haven the heroes will trust. Foolishly. Surround it with teeth.
A beacon placed, paired with a fountain, little king. A tempting little oasis. Make the oasis a trap.
A Soul-Bound Beacon. It LOOKS like mercy. Hngh — the dungeon does not do mercy. It does bait.

phylactery_heart.t: The Phylactery Heart. Your hidden spare life — place it where no hero would ever think to look. Hide it WELL.
The Heart is placed, little king. A fourth life, beating in the dark. Guard it like the secret it is.
Your Phylactery Heart. While it beats, death is a mere inconvenience. Lose it carelessly and... hngh. Do not.
